listing.name: Grallagh Castle
listing.desc: Grallagh Castle is a tower house and National Monument located in County Tipperary, Ireland.

imageAttribution.description: Castles of Munster: Grallagh, Tipperary Now in the care of the state, Grallagh stands close to the road between the town of Horse & Jockey and Ballinure. Rising above a steeply battered base, this four storeyed C16 tower house has exceptionally fine moulded two light windows at the upper levels, and bartizans on the SW and NE corners. The tower lies within a C16 bawn, whose remains consist of a 100 foot long section facing the road to the northeast.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Dunloe Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* kingdom of munster
*County:* County Kerry
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 55.21138889, -6.57916667
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dunluce_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Dunloe%20Castle/@52.0167,-9.65,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/04/Dunluce_Castle_Northern_Ireland_1.jpg/330px-Dunluce_Castle_Northern_Ireland_1.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* no
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* idk 

*Description:*
Dunluce Castle is a now-ruined medieval castle in Northern Ireland, the seat of Clan MacQuillan. It is located on the edge of a basalt outcropping in County Antrim, and is accessible via a bridge connecting it to the mainland. The castle is surrounded by extremely steep drops on either side, which may have been an important factor to the early Christians and Vikings who were drawn to this place where an early Irish fort once stood.

_Listing 2 of 186_

Leacanabuaile is a stone ringfort (cashel) and National Monument in County Kerry, Ireland. Leacanabuaile is immediately northwest of Cahergal, 3 km (1.9 mi) northwest of Cahirciveen.

*Name:* Ardglass Castles
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* ulster and the north
*County:* County Down
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 14th-15th century
*Coords:* 54.25833333, -5.60555556
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ardglass_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Ardglass%20Castles/@54.264,-5.604,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/ardglass-castles.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* idk 

*Description:*
Ardglass Castle is situated in Ardglass, County Down, Northern Ireland. It was originally a row of 15th century warehouses by the harbour. Large sections of the original building can still be seen within the modern club house of Ardglass Golf Club.

_Listing 7 of 186_
